title: Test cases should use multi-arch images
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST?**:

/ **kind bug**

**Description**


Test cases shipped with libpod for testing [containers](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/test/test_podman_baseline.sh) and [pods](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/test/test_podman_pods.sh) fail on architectures other than x64 (e.g. `ppc64le`). This is because the container images used in those test cases are not multi-arch. 

Images in question,

- [registry.access.redhat.com/rhscl/redis-32-rhel7](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/test/test_podman_baseline.sh#L63)

- [docker/whalesay:latest](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/test/test_podman_baseline.sh#L440)

- [quay.io/baude/alpine_nginx:latest](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/test/test_podman_pods.sh#L63)

- [fedora-minimal:28](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/test/test_podman_pods.sh#L64)


**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. https://github.com/containers/libpod/ (make sure you have installed `podman` and `buildah`)

2. run `test/test_podman_baseline.sh` and `test/test_podman_pods.sh` on an architecture other than x64, such as `ppc64le`


**Describe the results you received:**
For the every image in question above, you will get `standard_init_linux.go:203: exec user process caused "exec format error"`

**Describe the results you expected:**


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**
```bash
[root@rhel8 libpod]# podman version 
Version:       0.10.1.3
Go Version:    go1.10.3
OS/Arch:       linux/ppc64le
```


**Output of `podman info`:**

```
[root@rhel8 libpod]# podman info
host:
  BuildahVersion: 1.5-dev
  Conmon:
    package: podman-0.10.1.3-5.gitdb08685.el8+2131+7e3e9e07.ppc64le
    path: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 1.12.0-dev, commit: 6a63dada254d8efb3e9b30ed93ad55ab6622bd29-dirty'
  Distribution:
    distribution: '"rhel"'
    version: "8.0"
  MemFree: 2879324160
  MemTotal: 3722903552
  OCIRuntime:
    package: runc-1.0.0-52.rc5.dev.git2abd837.el8+1884+9fee228c.ppc64le
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: 'runc version spec: 1.0.0'
  SwapFree: 4382982144
  SwapTotal: 4395565056
  arch: ppc64le
  cpus: 1
  hostname: rhel8
  kernel: 4.18.0-40.el8.ppc64le
  os: linux
  uptime: 49h 57m 45.99s (Approximately 2.04 days)
insecure registries:
  registries: []
registries:
  registries:
  - registry.redhat.io
  - quay.io
  - docker.io
store:
  ContainerStore:
    number: 14
  GraphDriverName: overlay
  GraphOptions:
  - overlay.override_kernel_check=true
  GraphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage
  GraphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: xfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "true"
    Supports d_type: "true"
  ImageStore:
    number: 0
  RunRoot: /var/run/containers/storage

```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
arch - `ppc64le`

comments.body: I should mention that after replacing the default images with the standard multi-arch images everything worked on `ppc64le`, except for `docker/whalesay:latest`. I wasn't able to find a multi-arch image for `docker/whalesay:latest`. But the aim of that test case seemed like just printing something on the stdout, so I used standard `ubuntu:latest` and replaced CMD [arg](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/test/test_podman_baseline.sh#L442) with `cat /etc/hosts`
@baude Let me know if I could send the patch to address this issue.

@harche thanks for the problem report.  Always happy to get a PR spun up.  As far as the whale-says test goes, please do not delete that.  However if there's not a ppc64le image available and you've a way to not run it automatically in a ppc64le environment, I'd be fine with that.

You're right about it's use as an easy visual to see if the test has stopped running and at least that test passed.  However it's also has found several issues in the past with both Buildah and Podman during our pre-release testing so I'd very much like to keep it in play.

@TomSweeneyRedHat it looks like `docker/whalesay:latest` executes a perl command. So this should be easy to make it work on other architectures. I will create a multi-arch image which should work everywhere.
@harche can you swing into #podman on freenode and perhaps we can talk about this?  I'd like to talk over what you think the solution should be.

@baude The solution is to,

1. Create images for every arch using [this](https://github.com/docker/whalesay/blob/master/Dockerfile) Dockerfile. 
2. Then create a multi-arch image using `docker` or https://github.com/estesp/manifest-tool
3.  and finally refer that image in test case [here](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/test/test_podman_baseline.sh#L440).
fwiw, the whalesay tests are not an official part of our testing.  it is something we use manually (or in some cases programatically) to test the latest podman packages.

I will be making sure the `podman` packages work fine on `ppc64le`. So it would be helpful to me if all these tests pass on `ppc64le` too. 

Apart from these, do you have any other test suites?
